included: committed continuity modules and synthetic tests

contract.verification_first: The exact checked-out commit and immutable queue corpus must pass verifyEffectiveQueueArtifact before selection.
contract.ownership: Only items whose owner exactly equals the requesting team are eligible.
contract.status: Only active and queued items are eligible; completed, superseded, cancelled, blocked, and unknown statuses are not selected.
contract.dependency_rule: Every declared dependency must exist and have a terminal status before an item is actionable.
contract.ordering: Eligible items are ordered by numeric priority and then stable item id.
contract.failure_mode: Absent artifacts, invalid artifacts, unknown owners, no actionable owned work, and unresolved dependencies fail closed with reason codes.

observed: M-013 explicitly handed off creation of a startup adapter that verifies the generated effective queue before returning one owned item.
The selector module and adversarial test suite are committed.
The continuity workflow now includes the selector and its tests in path triggers and the fail-closed test command.
inferred: Teams invoking this adapter cannot silently select work from a stale, tampered, cross-commit, foreign-owner, or dependency-blocked queue artifact.
proposed: Each autonomous team runtime should invoke this adapter with its exact registered owner before any implementation work begins.
superseded: Selecting the first visually plausible queue record without independent artifact verification.
Treating owner labels or priority alone as sufficient actionability evidence.
unresolved: No successful GitHub Actions run is claimed for workflow commit 500bb34ef62ed47fcf990710170530e0d0f31d8c.
The four peer runtime prompts have not yet been changed to invoke the selector directly.
